title: What I think about Bars - Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle discusses bars and nightclubs in response to a viewer request, covering bar culture in Europe and Colorado mining towns, how bars serve as communal gathering places, and his predictions that bars/nightclubs will evolve into informal community centers with more individual expression in dancing. He is visibly frustrated, noting this is his third attempt to record the video due to iMovie issues and a computer crash.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: interesting part of European/mining culture but a temporary activity, not sustainable long-term
good places to see different people and dancing styles; will evolve toward outdoor/nature settings
will be replaced by informal local community centers where people gather without paying
in the future people will embrace individual originality rather than conforming to regional styles
pointless and something people will grow out of as society becomes more open

claims.subject: iTunes/iMovie
claims.claim: The way iTunes or iMovie works is stupid because if you record a bunch of videos, they get thrown into a pool of videos where you can't see them directly, only frames that you have to scroll through forever.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle hasn't had the time to figure out how to use Premier, so he's going to do a couple more videos with iTunes or iMovie before switching to Premier.

claims.subject: Kyle's computer
claims.claim: Kyle's computer crashed big time, and now he has to take it to the Apple Store, hoping he doesn't need to get a new computer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is not in the greatest mood but is determined to finish the video for the third attempt.

claims.subject: bars
claims.claim: Bars are part of the culture of Europe, and when Europeans came to America in the 1800s for mining, they brought their bar culture with them.

claims.subject: bars in Boulder
claims.claim: Boulder has a bar culture influenced by its concentration of microbreweries.

claims.subject: bars in Europe
claims.claim: In Europe, local bars are cozy places where people gather regularly, and you can tell outsiders because they are unfamiliar faces.

claims.subject: bars in mining towns
claims.claim: In mining towns, bars were common, and people would go to saloons to drink, which was a way to bring people together.

claims.subject: bars
claims.claim: Bars are a temporary activity and not something sustainable for the long term, and they might be replaced by general get-together places in the future.

claims.subject: night clubs
claims.claim: Night clubs are places where people gather to dance in different styles, and in the future, they might be more outdoors, like beach night clubs in Alaska with heated water.

claims.subject: dancing styles
claims.claim: In South America, women dance by moving their hips, while in America, people dance with their hands up.

claims.subject: dancing in the future
claims.claim: In the future, people will dance differently, with more originality, and making fun of others for their dancing will be less common.

claims.subject: communal living
claims.claim: In the future, communal living might replace bars with local community centers where people gather informally without paying anything.

claims.subject: drugs
claims.claim: In the future, if someone wants to do drugs and freak out, they will do it at the local community center instead of at home.
